I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_OMI_HEALTH_carbon_ph_trend 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
Secular trends and the associated uncertainties ( ±1σ) of seawater pH over the 
global surface ocean are computed from the 100 -member ensemble of pH time 
series. This ensemble covering the period since 1985 was randomly regenerated 
with a Gaussian distribution where the mean and the standard deviation are, 
respectively, the best estimate and the total uncertainty of pH available in the 
Surface Carbon field MULTIOBS_GLO_BIO_CARBON_SURFACE_REP_015_008 
(Chau et al., 2023a). Surface ocean pH is reported on total scale and the units of 
its trend and uncertainty are yr -1 in numerical data files. For a concise 
presentation, this document sh ows the units of pH tre nds and uncertainty in 
decade-1. 
Denote µij and σij as a linear trend and its uncertainty computed from a batch of 
100 yearly mean pH time series (pH t,ij,n) at each 0.25°×0.25° -grid cell ij, where t 
stands for time and n indicates a member in the 100-member ensemble. A linear 
least-squares regression was used to fit a linear function between two sets of data 
(i.e., pH t,ij,n against t values) and derive estimates of its slope (µ ij) and residual 
standard deviation (σij).  An illustration of the method of linear fits on the pH data 
at some stations is shown in Figure 1. 
The global maps of pH trend and uncertainty are presented in Figure 2 (a,b). A 
threshold on 
𝜎
|𝜇| of  10% is empirically defined to mask the regions with uncertain
